title: Broken Syntax
story: [Meridian-7 Log #007] Structured identifiers have begun appearing in rEx's data. Some start with a letter or underscore, followed by alphanumeric combinations — these resemble "variable names" in rEx's language. But many invalid formats are mixed in. You need to learn to distinguish them.
promptChallenge: [Target] Valid identifiers — names that start with a letter or underscore.
hints: [Hint] The first character of a variable name is restricted
[Hint] Digits cannot be the starting character
promptHints: Describe the rules for variable names: what characters can start them
Tell the AI to exclude names starting with digits or special symbols
Clarify which characters can appear in variable names

feedback.onPass: [Pattern Accepted] Syntax structure recognized. rEx's variable naming follows a pattern.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Syntax parsing failed. Pay attention to the first-character constraint.
feedback.onPerfect: [Decryption Success] Perfect parsing. The power of character classes is emerging.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual syntax parsing. You know character classes inside out.
